title: Schiedea trinervis
taxonomy: Schiedea trinervis is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Schiedea, which is a group of flowering plants endemic to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant is further classified as a member of the order Caryophyllales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Schiedea trinervis is a rare and endangered species, found only in a few locations on the island of Hawaii. Its classification as a unique and endemic species highlights the importance of conservation efforts to protect its habitat and genetic diversity.

intro: Schiedea trinervis, also known as the Hawaiian violet or Ma'o hau hele, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae that is endemic to Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Schiedea trinervis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Schiedea trinervis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential health benefits and cultural significance.
